ATTENTION INBOX WORK (one item per Mode 2b protocol):
  Item: ATT-2026-04-12T14:42:49.149Z (target_type=section, target_id=SEC-1.8, pushed_by=decider, related ISS-780/ISS-781)
  Reason: After ISS-780 patched 'eight orders of magnitude' → 'more than six orders' in sections.json, verify rest of n(r)/Gaia quantitative framing is still accurate, and integrate chromaticity bridge (EXP-096) when ready.

  VERIFICATION FINDINGS:
  (1) ISS-780 closed=fixed. ISS-781 closed=fixed. EXP-096 (chromaticity bridge) integrated 2026-04-13 — confirmed in SEC-1.8 detail prose verbatim. Mode 2b protocol's 'integrate chromaticity bridge' instruction is satisfied.
  (2) Quantitative anchors verified accurate: 300–6,000× refractive ratio bounds (273–6,280 against atmosphere n−1=0.000293); 920× at TOC; 2,690× at Sydney; 1.8 billion Gaia stars; factor 5,200 for solar diameter; n(r) values 1.08 / 1.27 / 1.44 / 1.83 / 2.84 against the dome formula n(r)=1+0.20×(8537/H(r)−1) with H(r)=8537·exp(−r/8619).
  (3) TWO ERRORS FOUND:
      • SEC-1.8 prose: '~10 microarcseconds (0.00000278°)'. Arithmetic: 10 μas = 10×10⁻⁶ arcsec = 10⁻⁵ arcsec; 1 arcsec = 1°/3600 ≈ 2.78×10⁻⁴°; therefore 10 μas = 2.78×10⁻⁹° (i.e. 0.00000000278°). Current text gives 0.00000278° which is 10 milliarcseconds — off by 1000×. Patch: parenthetical → '(≈2.78 × 10⁻⁹°, i.e. about a billionth of a degree)'.
      • WIN-016 detail_evidence: same 'eight orders of magnitude' claim ISS-780 fixed in sections.json was never propagated to wins.json. Patch: 'eight' → 'more than six' (matches ISS-780's resolution and curmudgeon arithmetic n−1 = 0.235 / 1e−7 ≈ 2.35e6 ≈ 6.4 orders).
  (4) WIN-069's 'eight orders of magnitude' (4,000 km baseline geodesy context) is arithmetically correct and explicitly NOT touched (verified before-string uniqueness via grep: WIN-016's exact sentence 'incompatible with Gaia DR3 microarcsecond astrometry by eight orders of magnitude' occurs once in wins.json).

  USER-PREFERENCE DIRECTIVE (Think about the other side too):
   (a) Could the parenthetical 0.00000278° be intentional mas-scale? — REJECTED, sentence explicitly anchors on 'microarcseconds' and Gaia's actual precision (~10–25 μas) supports the unit.
   (b) Does 'more than six' weaken the WIN-016 argument vs 'eight'? — Marginally, but argument is robust to the change; six-order excess is still catastrophic. Aligning with ISS-780's resolution is the priority.
   (c) Should we tighten 'more than six' to a specific value (e.g. '6.4')? — REJECTED, ISS-780 deliberately settled on qualitative phrasing because the exact figure depends on r-position and Gaia threshold choice.
